item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Narrow Road to the Deep North
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
Burma Railway in 1943 and across the Pacific during World War II, charts the cruelty of war, the tenuousness of life and the impossibility of love, as seen through the eyes of an Australian doctor and prisoner of war.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: Best of the Worst: Star Slammer, Talos, and The Apple
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded:
Ahoy! It’s time for an epic Best of the Worst episode filled with magic, martial arts, and Menahem! Menahem Golan that is, of Cannon Films, as we celebrate the sci fi dystopian musical The Apple! We also watched some disgusting trash called Star Slammer about a women’s prison in space. It’s filth. Utter filth. What would Jesus do? And to round things out, we watch the ambitious no budget martial arts fantasy adventure film Talos. Look out everyone, it’s Talos!
Special thanks to Bear McCreary (https://bearmccreary.com) for joining us on this nightmare.

item.title: When an Actress Stopped Playing It Safe – Emma Stone
item.author: FilmStack
item.content:encoded:
In the early 2010s, Emma Stone was everywhere. With Superbad, Zombieland, and Easy A, she became the poster girl for the witty love interest or quirky lead, continuing to similar roles like in Crazy Stupid Love and The Amazing Spider-Man. But for her, that wasn’t enough.
The roles kept coming, but not the chances to grow. So instead of playing it safe, she chased tougher roles like in The Help and Birdman, with one even leaving her wondering if she was even good enough. But she didn’t back down. She pushed further, taking on live theatre, which became the hardest thing she’s ever done.
Those risks laid the foundation for La La Land, a turning point that changed how the industry saw her. And instead of coasting after that, she doubled down by choosing weirder, bolder films. The Favourite. Cruella. Poor Things. Kinds of Kindness. Bugonia. Not just acting in them, but producing them too.
This is the story of an actress who refuses to settle, and quietly built one of the most exciting careers in Hollywood. This is Emma Stone.
Chapters: 00:00 - Intro 00:45 - Project Hollywood 04:20 - The Shift 08:48 - No More Playing It Safe 13:27 - In Full Control
Emma Stone. Superbad. The Rocker. The House Bunny. Zombieland. Jonah Hill. Jesse Eisenberg. Easy A. Crazy Stupid Love. The Help. The Amazing Spiderman. Andrew Garfield. Gwen Stacy. The Croods. Birdman. Aloha. Woody Allen. La La Land. Damien Chazelle. Billie Jean King. Yorgos Lanthimos. The Favourite. Cruella. Poor Things. Kinds of Kindness. Eddington. Ari Aster. Bugonia.
If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.
If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.
Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/
#emmastone #actress #lalaland

item.title: First Take: Superman (2025) | Movie Review
item.author: Layton Eversaul
item.content:encoded:
My review of James Gunn's "Superman" (2025), starring David Corenswet, Rachel Brosnahan and Nicholas Hoult.
NOW ON PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/LaytonEversaul Threads: https://www.threads.com/@LaytonEversaul Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/LaytonReviews Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/laytoneversaul

item.title: Half in the Bag: Superman (2025)
item.author: RedLetterMedia
item.content:encoded: Have you ever heard of Superman? Have you ever seen a Superman movie? Well, there's another one now. It's the newest one. There have been Superman movies and now this is one of them. This one is directed by James Gunn. The previous one was directed by Zack Snyder. The two of them worked together a long time ago on the Dawn of the Dead remake. The original Dawn of the Dead was filmed in Pittsburgh. Pittsburgh is the second most populated city in the state of Pennsylvania. It is commonly knows as the Steel City. Superman is often called the Man of Steel. Man of Steel is a movie that was directed by Zack Snyder. But this new Superman movie is not directed by Zack Snyder.

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Superman
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
Superman must reconcile his alien Kryptonian heritage with his human upbringing as reporter Clark Kent. As the embodiment of truth, justice and the human way he soon finds himself in a world that views these as old-fashioned.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.title: Mark Kermode reviews Modì, Three Days on the Wing of Madness
item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
Through the streets of World War I Paris, Italian bohemian artist Modigliani attempts to out maneuver bombs, policemen, girlfriends, and critics in a increasingly desperate search of a home for his art.
To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://podfollow.com/1616559297

item.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
item.content:encoded:
#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #FilmReviews
Gabriel Book, proprietor of an antiquarian bookshop, relies on his vast collection to unravel baffling cases. He nurtures a group of lovable yet troubled individuals, providing informal protection and guidance.

item.title: The Story of Superman: The Movie (1978)
item.author: Layton Eversaul
item.content:encoded:
In this in-depth and feature-length retrospective, I tell the story behind cinema's first blockbuster comic book movie: "Superman: The Movie" (1978). From its shaky development to its innovated but troubled production, from its massive marketing campaign to its generation-spanning legacy, I reveal the true story behind the making of a groundbreaking classic. Directed by Richard Donner. Starring: Christopher Reeve, Margot Kidder, Marlon Brando, and Gene Hackman.
NOW ON PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/LaytonEversaul Threads: https://www.threads.com/@LaytonEversaul Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/LaytonReviews Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/laytoneversaul
